{"id":"smallhold-x4mh","title":"Schema and endpoints for GET/POST/DELETE /api/v1/followed_tags","description":"## What
Create `followed_hashtags` table via migration: (id PK snowflake, persona_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES personas(id), hashtag TEXT NOT NULL, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL, UNIQUE(persona_id, hashtag)). Implement three endpoints: GET /api/v1/followed_tags — return paginated list of followed Tag objects with `following: true`; POST /api/v1/followed_tags — body `{"name": "rust"}`, normalize tag (lowercase, strip leading #), insert row, return Tag with `following: true`; DELETE /api/v1/followed_tags/:name — unfollow by hashtag name, return 200 with Tag object.

## Why
Clients need these endpoints to let users manage their followed hashtags list. The endpoint is /api/v1/followed_tags (not /api/v1/tags/:name/follow — that is a different, older pattern) — using the wrong path breaks client integration.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social GET /api/v1/followed_tags to capture exact Tag object shape with the `following` field; curl POST /api/v1/followed_tags to see request body format and response; curl DELETE /api/v1/followed_tags/:name to see response shape; note that the endpoint is /followed_tags not /tags/:name/follow
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write migration for followed_hashtags table with UNIQUE constraint; implement GET handler with since_id/max_id/limit pagination via Link header; implement POST handler normalizing hashtag name (strip #, lowercase), inserting with IGNORE on conflict, returning Tag; implement DELETE handler removing row by persona_id+hashtag, returning Tag with following:false
- Spawn a Test subagent to: POST follow 'rust', assert Tag response shape; GET /followed_tags and assert 'rust' appears; POST follow 'rust' again (duplicate) and assert 200 not 409; DELETE unfollow 'rust' and assert following:false; GET and assert list is empty; test 404 on DELETE of non-followed tag

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social live /api/v1/followed_tags and /api/v1/followed_tags (POST) responses — capture exact Tag object shape including `following` boolean field, `name`, `url`, `history` array
- Test approach: integration test full follow/list/unfollow lifecycle; assert Tag response fields match mastodon.social shape; assert UNIQUE constraint (duplicate follow is idempotent not error); assert pagination Link header on GET when multiple tags followed

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Normalize hashtag on POST: strip leading #, lowercase, trim whitespace; reject empty string with 422
- Scope all queries to authenticated persona_id — never expose another persona's followed tags
- UNIQUE(persona_id, hashtag) in DB prevents duplicates; handler should treat conflict as success (idempotent)
- DELETE by name (not by id) — look up by persona_id + hashtag text","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:20:18Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:20:18Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-chxy","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:20:38Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
